content: Postgres advisory locks: session-level (pg_advisory_lock) held until explicit release or session end; transaction-level (pg_advisory_xact_lock) released at transaction end. For a long-lived process holding a lock across a streamer session, SESSION-LEVEL is correct — it survives multiple transactions. pg_try_advisory_lock returns bool (non-blocking attempt). CRITICAL: session-level advisory locks are incompatible with PgBouncer transaction-mode pooler — the session is returned to pool between transactions, releasing the lock. Must use direct/session-pooler connection (Supabase port 5432) for the advisory lock connection. The sidecar needs a dedicated long-lived direct DB connection for the advisory lock, separate from any pooled connections.
